title: [InstCombine] Extend Fold of Zero-extended Bit Test

tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "n64"

define i32 @zext_masked_bit_constant_shift_amount(i32 %a) {
  %shl = shl i32 1, 5
  %and = and i32 %shl, %a
  %cmp = icmp ne i32 %and, 0
  %z = zext i1 %cmp to i32
  ret i32 %z
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions variable shift amounts via 'shl i32 1, %b' but does not explicitly cover constant shift amounts like 'shl i32 1, 5'. The patch handles both, but constant shifts are outside the documented scope.

alive2.src:
target datalayout = "n64"

define i32 @zext_masked_bit_constant_shift_amount(i32 %a) {
  %shl = shl i32 1, 5
  %and = and i32 %shl, %a
  %cmp = icmp ne i32 %and, 0
  %z = zext i1 %cmp to i32
  ret i32 %z
}
alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"
target datalayout = "n64"

define i32 @zext_masked_bit_constant_shift_amount(i32 %a) {
  %and = lshr i32 %a, 5
  %and.lobit = and i32 %and, 1
  ret i32 %and.lobit
}
alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i32 @zext_masked_bit_constant_shift_amount(i32 %a) {
#0:
  %shl = shl i32 1, 5
  %and = and i32 %shl, %a
  %cmp = icmp ne i32 %and, 0
  %z = zext i1 %cmp to i32
  ret i32 %z
}
=>
define i32 @zext_masked_bit_constant_shift_amount(i32 %a) {
#0:
  %and = lshr i32 %a, 5
  %and.lobit = and i32 %and, 1
  ret i32 %and.lobit
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "n64"

define i128 @zext_masked_bit_i128_bitwidth(i128 %a, i128 %b) {
  %shl = shl i128 1, %b
  %and = and i128 %shl, %a
  %cmp = icmp eq i128 %and, 0
  %z = zext i1 %cmp to i128
  ret i128 %z
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
target datalayout = "n64"

define i128 @zext_masked_bit_i128_bitwidth(i128 %a, i128 %b) {
  %not = xor i128 %a, -1
  %lshr = lshr i128 %not, %b
  %and = and i128 %lshr, 1
  ret i128 %and
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary and existing tests cover i16, i32, i64 bitwidths but do not mention i128. The patch's CreateZExtOrTrunc handles arbitrary bitwidths including i128, which is outside the documented scope.

alive2.src:
target datalayout = "n64"

define i128 @zext_masked_bit_i128_bitwidth(i128 %a, i128 %b) {
  %shl = shl i128 1, %b
  %and = and i128 %shl, %a
  %cmp = icmp eq i128 %and, 0
  %z = zext i1 %cmp to i128
  ret i128 %z
}
alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"
target datalayout = "n64"

define i128 @zext_masked_bit_i128_bitwidth(i128 %a, i128 %b) {
  %1 = xor i128 %a, -1
  %2 = lshr i128 %1, %b
  %z = and i128 %2, 1
  ret i128 %z
}

tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "n64"

define i32 @zext_masked_bit_i8_source_type(i8 %a, i8 %b) {
  %shl = shl i8 1, %b
  %and = and i8 %shl, %a
  %cmp = icmp ne i8 %and, 0
  %z = zext i1 %cmp to i32
  ret i32 %z
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
target datalayout = "n64"

define i32 @zext_masked_bit_i8_source_type(i8 %a, i8 %b) {
  %lshr = lshr i8 %a, %b
  %and = and i8 %lshr, 1
  %zext = zext i8 %and to i32
  ret i32 %zext
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Existing tests use i32 as the source type for the masked bit test. The patch supports arbitrary source bitwidths including i8, but this specific small integer source type is not mentioned in the summary.

alive2.src:
target datalayout = "n64"

define i32 @zext_masked_bit_i8_source_type(i8 %a, i8 %b) {
  %shl = shl i8 1, %b
  %and = and i8 %shl, %a
  %cmp = icmp ne i8 %and, 0
  %z = zext i1 %cmp to i32
  ret i32 %z
}
alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"
target datalayout = "n64"

define i32 @zext_masked_bit_i8_source_type(i8 %a, i8 %b) {
  %1 = lshr i8 %a, %b
  %2 = and i8 %1, 1
  %z = zext nneg i8 %2 to i32
  ret i32 %z
}
